# Wazuh SIEM Cheat Sheet
**CYART SOC Team | Week 2**

---

## Installation (Server — Ubuntu 22.04)

```bash
# 1. Download and run Wazuh installer
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.7/wazuh-install.sh
sudo bash wazuh-install.sh -a

# 2. Save credentials printed at end of install
# Default Wazuh Dashboard: https://<SERVER_IP>
# Username: admin
# Password: <auto-generated>

# 3. Check service status
sudo systemctl status wazuh-manager
sudo systemctl status wazuh-dashboard
sudo systemctl status wazuh-indexer
```

---

## Agent Installation (Windows)

```powershell
# Run in PowerShell as Administrator
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/windows/wazuh-agent-4.7.0-1.msi -OutFile wazuh-agent.msi

msiexec.exe /i wazuh-agent.msi /q WAZUH_MANAGER="<MANAGER_IP>" WAZUH_AGENT_NAME="Windows-Endpoint"

# Start service
NET START WazuhSvc
```

## Agent Installation (Linux)

```bash
# Add Wazuh repo
curl -s https://packages.wazuh.com/key/GPG-KEY-WAZUH | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/wazuh.gpg] https://packages.wazuh.com/4.x/apt/ stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/wazuh.list

sudo apt-get update
sudo WAZUH_MANAGER="<MANAGER_IP>" apt-get install wazuh-agent -y
sudo systemctl enable --now wazuh-agent
```

---

## Wazuh Dashboard Access
- URL: `https://<SERVER_IP>` (port 443)
- Default credentials set during install

---

## Wazuh Configuration Files

```bash
# Manager config
/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf

# Agent config
/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf  (on agent)

# Custom rules
/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml

# Decoders
/var/ossec/etc/decoders/local_decoder.xml

# Active response
/var/ossec/active-response/bin/
```

---

## AlienVault OTX Integration

### Step 1: Get OTX API Key
```
1. Register at https://otx.alienvault.com
2. Go to Settings → API Integration
3. Copy your OTX Key
```

### Step 2: Install OTX Integration Script
```bash
# Install Python and otxv2 library
sudo apt install python3-pip -y
pip3 install OTXv2

# Clone Wazuh OTX integration
cd /var/ossec/integrations/
sudo wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/socfortress/Wazuh-Rules/main/OTX/custom-otx.py
sudo chmod +x custom-otx.py
```

### Step 3: Configure Integration in ossec.conf
```xml
<!-- Add inside <ossec_config> in /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf -->
<integration>
  <name>custom-otx</name>
  <api_key>YOUR_OTX_API_KEY_HERE</api_key>
  <hook_url>https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/indicators/</hook_url>
  <alert_format>json</alert_format>
  <level>5</level>
</integration>
```

### Step 4: Restart Wazuh Manager
```bash
sudo systemctl restart wazuh-manager
sudo tail -f /var/ossec/logs/ossec.log
```

---

## Custom Detection Rules

### Rule for T1078 — Valid Accounts
```xml
<!-- /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml -->
<group name="authentication,valid_accounts">
  <rule id="100001" level="10">
    <if_group>authentication_success</if_group>
    <match>type=USER_LOGIN</match>
    <description>T1078: Valid Accounts - Successful login detected</description>
    <mitre>
      <id>T1078</id>
    </mitre>
  </rule>

  <rule id="100002" level="14">
    <if_sid>100001</if_sid>
    <time>19:00 - 07:00</time>
    <description>T1078: Valid Accounts - After-hours login attempt</description>
    <group>after_hours_login</group>
  </rule>
</group>
```

### Rule for PowerShell Execution
```xml
<rule id="100010" level="12">
  <if_group>sysmon</if_group>
  <field name="win.eventdata.image">powershell.exe</field>
  <field name="win.eventdata.commandLine">EncodedCommand|DownloadString|IEX|bypass</field>
  <description>T1059.001: Suspicious PowerShell execution detected</description>
  <mitre>
    <id>T1059.001</id>
  </mitre>
</rule>
```

Reload rules without restart:
```bash
sudo /var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control reload
```

---

## Threat Hunting Queries (Wazuh API)

```bash
# Authenticate and get token
TOKEN=$(curl -su admin:SecretPassword -k -X GET "https://localhost:55000/security/user/authenticate?raw=true")

# Get all alerts (last 24h)
curl -k -X GET "https://localhost:55000/security/events" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json"

# Hunt for T1078 — filter by user != system
curl -k -X GET "https://localhost:55000/security/events?q=data.win.system.eventID=4624;data.win.eventdata.targetUserName!=system" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

# Search by IP
curl -k -X GET "https://localhost:55000/security/events?q=data.srcip=192.168.1.100" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

---

## Wazuh Active Response — Block IP with CrowdSec

```bash
# Install CrowdSec
curl -s https://packagecloud.io/install/repositories/crowdsec/crowdsec/script.deb.sh | sudo bash
sudo apt-get install crowdsec -y
sudo apt install crowdsec-firewall-bouncer-iptables -y

# Block specific IP manually
sudo cscli decisions add --ip 192.168.1.101 --duration 24h --reason "Samba exploit attempt"

# List blocked IPs
sudo cscli decisions list

# Remove a block
sudo cscli decisions delete --ip 192.168.1.101
```

---

## Useful Wazuh Commands

```bash
# Manager status and logs
sudo systemctl status wazuh-manager
sudo tail -f /var/ossec/logs/ossec.log
sudo tail -f /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json

# List connected agents
sudo /var/ossec/bin/manage_agents -l

# Restart manager
sudo systemctl restart wazuh-manager

# Test custom rules
sudo /var/ossec/bin/ossec-logtest
```
